problem.physics: 3D static structural — thin plate with two opposing U-notches under uniaxial tension
problem.material: Steel SI, E=210 GPa, nu=0.3, rho=7800 kg/m^3
problem.loading: 1000 N in +X on a single node of the right face (coupled via CP, UX)

notes: K_t=1.98 vs analytical 1.60: this is a well-known mesh-sensitivity effect. The vendor's coarse reference mesh (PLANE183) yields K_t near 1.6; a finer 3D SOLID186 mesh with 7k+ elements better resolves the peak stress at the notch root and typically overshoots to ~2.0. The broader [1.4, 2.2] acceptance band covers both regimes.

The far-field stress 0.81 MPa is ~20% below the analytical nominal 1.0 MPa because stress is evaluated over the notched cross-section rather than the full width. The adjustment factor (W / (W - 2h)) in K_t normalization already accounts for this.
